BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Quick back-of-napkin after Jacob mentioned the landlord might pass through a property tax bump on the 1402 Williamson St lease. Pre year-end review.

CurrentState.Note: Rough estimate only. Grain (corn/rye/malted barley), TTB excise tax, and barrel/cooperage costs roll into Supplies and Bottling here; need to revisit with Derek once 2025 actuals close. Bottle club not yet broken out separately.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Rent increases to $5,500/mo if property tax pass-through hits
Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: Fixed/variable costs $38,900 + $500 increase = $39,400. At ~$14 average tasting-room ticket plus wholesale/online mix, $39,400 / $14 = 2,814.3 → ~2,815 equivalent tasting-room transactions/mo, well within current 1,100 tasting + wholesale + online volume.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Minimal. Plenty of buffer against the ~$520K annual run rate.

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Worst case - rent to $6,000/mo (unlikely per landlord)
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Still fine. Landlord said this was unlikely but Jacob wanted to stress test the Williamson St lease.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: Break-even sits around $39K/mo total cost with grain, TTB excise tax, barrels/cooperage, payroll for 8 employees, utilities, and bottling/packaging factored in. At ~$43K/mo revenue across tasting room, wholesale, online/direct, and bottle club we have headroom. Main concern is the winter tasting-room dip, but wholesale and online carry us through.
ActionItems: Wait for the landlord to confirm the property tax pass-through on 1402 Williamson St
Revisit after year-end with Derek Holmgren for proper modeling, including a separate bottle-club line
